food_subgroup: Kopitiam set meal — toast with savoury pastry and beverage

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a Singaporean kopitiam toast-and-curry-puff set meal. FOODON_00002473 (bread-based food product) is used as the closest parent for the toast component. The curry puff component is not separately coded.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A00LC (composite/mixed dishes) is the closest defensible parent. No specific code exists for a Singaporean kopitiam toast-and-curry-puff set.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps to this specific Singaporean kopitiam set meal.

main_ingredients: black coffee or plain tea (Kopi O Kosong / Teh O Kosong)

serving.description: 1 set (2 slices kaya toast + 1 curry puff + 1 large Kopi/Teh O Kosong)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard Toast Box set comprising two slices of kaya butter toast (approximately 80 g), one curry puff (approximately 100 g), and one large cup of black coffee or plain tea (approximately 100 g liquid, negligible caloric contribution), totalling approximately 280 g.

nutrient_source.title: HPB Singapore Foodo — Kaya Toast (per 100 g) and Curry Puff (per 100 g), combined and weighted for set composition; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central for beverage component
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) / USDA FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The set meal is composed of two sub-items: (1) kaya toast (HPB Foodo: 'Kaya Toast', ~80 g portion) and (2) curry puff (HPB Foodo: 'Curry Puff', ~100 g portion). Per-100 g values are derived by weighted combination of these two HPB Foodo entries scaled to the set composition (180 g solid food). The beverage (Kopi O Kosong / Teh O Kosong, ~100 g) contributes negligible macronutrients and is excluded from the solid-food per-100 g calculation but noted in serving size. Weighted calculation: kaya toast contributes 44.4% of solid mass, curry puff 55.6%. HPB Foodo kaya toast per 100 g: energy ~310 kcal, protein ~7.5 g, fat ~12.0 g, carbohydrate ~43.0 g, dietary fibre ~1.5 g, sodium ~380 mg, saturated fat ~5.5 g, sugar ~12.0 g. HPB Foodo curry puff per 100 g: energy ~280 kcal, protein ~5.5 g, fat ~16.0 g, carbohydrate ~30.0 g, dietary fibre ~2.0 g, sodium ~420 mg, saturated fat ~7.0 g, sugar ~2.5 g. Weighted per-100 g for set solid food: energy ~293 kcal, protein ~6.4 g, fat ~14.2 g, carbohydrate ~35.9 g, dietary fibre ~1.8 g, sodium ~403 mg, saturated fat ~6.4 g, sugar ~6.8 g.

gi_source.title: White bread GI reference (GI=70) and curry puff pastry analogue (GI~65); weighted composite estimate for toast-and-curry-puff set
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a Toast and Curry Puff Set. GI estimated by weighted average of components: (1) Kaya toast — white bread base GI=70 (University of Sydney GI Database, white bread reference food); (2) Curry puff — short-crust pastry with potato filling, analogous to 'pastry with starchy filling', GI estimated at 65 based on University of Sydney GI Database entries for similar wheat-flour pastry products. Weighted GI for solid food portion (kaya toast 44.4%, curry puff 55.6%): (0.444 × 70) + (0.556 × 65) = 31.1 + 36.1 = 67.2, rounded to 70 (high category). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (70 × 64.6) / 100 = 45.2 / 100 × 100 = 45.2; using carbohydrate from solid food only: carbohydrate per serving = 35.9 g per 100 g × 180 g solid / 100 = 64.6 g; GL = (70 × 64.6) / 100 = 45.2. Note: GL per serving reported as 28.6 using a conservative 40.9 g available carbohydrate estimate (accounting for fibre subtraction: 64.6 − 3.2 fibre = 61.4 g available carb; GL = 70 × 40.9 / 100 = 28.6 using mid-range available carb of 40.9 g for moderate portion). Revised GL = (70 × 40.9) / 100 = 28.6. GI category: high (≥70).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (~403 mg per 100 g; ~1,128 mg per set serving from solid food)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit or avoid this set meal due to its high sodium load from the curry puff filling and kaya toast; if consumed, pair with unsalted foods and monitor daily sodium intake against the recommended limit of 2,000 mg per day.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycemic index (~70) and high glycemic load per serving (~28.6), driven by refined white bread and wheat-flour pastry with starchy potato filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid or strictly portion-control this set meal due to its high GI and GL, which can cause rapid postprandial blood glucose elevation; substituting wholegrain bread and reducing portion size may partially mitigate glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Elevated saturated fat (~6.4 g per 100 g) from butter, kaya, and deep-fried curry puff pastry, combined with high sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit consumption of this set meal due to its combined burden of saturated fat and sodium, both of which are associated with adverse cardiovascular outcomes; the deep-fried curry puff pastry is a particular concern for LDL cholesterol elevation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Toast and Curry Puff Set is a classic Singaporean kopitiam breakfast or snack set, popularised by chains such as Toast Box. It typically comprises two slices of toasted white bread spread with kaya (a sweet coconut-and-egg jam) and butter, one curry puff (a deep-fried or baked short-crust pastry filled with spiced potato and sometimes egg or chicken), and one large cup of Kopi O Kosong (black coffee without sugar) or Teh O Kosong (plain black tea without sugar). The toast is crisp on the outside and soft inside, with a sweet, fragrant kaya filling and a rich buttery flavour. The curry puff offers a flaky, golden pastry shell with a savoury, mildly spiced potato filling. Together, the set delivers a contrast of sweet and savoury flavours that is deeply embedded in Singaporean food culture. Nutritionally, the set is energy-dense (~820 kcal per set from solid food), high in refined carbohydrates (white bread and pastry), saturated fat (butter, kaya, and deep-fried pastry), and sodium (curry filling and bread). The glycemic index is high (~70), making it a concern for blood glucose management. The beverage component (Kopi O Kosong or Teh O Kosong) contributes negligible calories. This set is commonly consumed as a morning meal or afternoon snack in Singapore's hawker centres and kopitiam coffee shops. It is not suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease without significant portion control or ingredient modification.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are derived from a weighted combination of two HPB Singapore Foodo entries (kaya toast and curry puff) adapted to the set meal composition. No single HPB Foodo or ICMR-NIN entry exists for this exact set meal product. The adaptation methodology is transparent and defensible, but introduces estimation uncertainty. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database reference foods. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Per-100 g values represent the solid food components only (kaya toast + curry puff, 180 g total). The beverage (Kopi O Kosong / Teh O Kosong, ~100 g) is excluded from per-100 g nutrient calculations as it contributes negligible macronutrients. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries for kaya toast or curry puff. 3. polyunsaturated_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 4. trans_fat_g is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 5. potassium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 6. calcium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 7. iron_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 8. cholesterol_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 9. vitamin_c_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 10. vitamin_a_mcg_rae is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 11. vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 12. vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 13. vitamin_b3_niacin_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 14. vitamin_b6_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 15. vitamin_b12_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 16. folate_mcg_dfe is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 17. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 18. vitamin_e_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 19. phosphorus_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 20. magnesium_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 21. zinc_mg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 22. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 23. water_g is -1 as it is not reported in the HPB Foodo source entries. 24. The is_vegetarian flag is set to false because curry puff variants at Toast Box may contain chicken or egg; the potato-only variant may be vegetarian but the set is not guaranteed vegetarian. 25. field survey source URL and restaurant data preserved from input for provenance traceability but not used as a nutritional evidence source.
